title: Warm Crunchy Mexican Lamb Elote
description: A briny, crunchy, Mexican dish featuring lamb loin chops, served in a vegetarian-friendly elote form, perfect for a brunch mood. This dish combines the flavors of Mexico with the tenderness of lamb.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together chili powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add the lamb pieces to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the lamb to a baking sheet and bake in the oven for 12 minutes, or until cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the lamb is cooking, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in the same skillet over medium heat.
Add the corn kernels to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until lightly browned and crispy, about 5 minutes.
Stir in mayonnaise, lime juice, and cotija cheese into the corn mixture.
To assemble the elote, place the cooked lamb on a serving dish, then top with the corn mixture.
